clues.clue: This red-haired clown debuted as the McDonald's mascot in 1963, first played by Willard Scott.
This monocled, top-hatted legume has represented Planters since 1916.
This giggling, doughy figure springs up when poked to sell Pillsbury products.
This towering verdant mascot booms 'Ho ho ho' for a frozen-vegetable brand.
clues.response: Who is the Jolly Green Giant?

clues.clue: This beret-wearing cartoon fish ironically keeps trying to get caught by a tuna brand.
Jafar's sarcastic, squawking sidekick in 'Aladdin' is this red parrot.
Ursula's two slippery eel minions are Flotsam and this one.
Captain Hook's anxious yet kindly first mate is this fellow.
Scar's hyena trio is Shenzi, Banzai and this non-speaking, giggling one.
In 'Sleeping Beauty,' Maleficent's clumsy pig-snouted foot soldiers are this horde.
Shakespeare gives this assassinated dictator the dying line 'Et tu, Brute?'
This patriot spy reportedly regretted having but one life to give before his 1776 hanging.
This German writer's reputed final words were 'More light!' ('Mehr Licht!').
This British admiral died at Trafalgar reportedly murmuring 'Kiss me, Hardy.'
This Australian outlaw reputedly faced the gallows in 1880 saying 'Such is life.'
Basketball's 'His Airness' won six NBA titles with the Chicago Bulls.
Boxing's 'The Greatest' lit the cauldron at the 1996 Atlanta Olympics.
Hockey's 'The Great One' holds the NHL career points record.
Soccer's 'O Rei' (The King), this Brazilian won three World Cups.
Baseball's 'The Splendid Splinter' was the last man to bat .400 in a season.
Milton Hershey built a model company town for his chocolate works in this state.
PEZ originated in this European country in 1927 as a peppermint for adults.
The second 'M' in M&M's honors Bruce, son of a Hershey president, whose surname is this.
Candy corn, dating to the 1880s, was originally marketed as 'Chicken' this.
This Quaker family firm, founded in Birmingham in 1824, makes Dairy Milk.

final.clue: This purpose-built modernist city replaced Rio de Janeiro as Brazil's capital in 1960.
